{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

==External links== *[https://www.city.miyoshi.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/35/3515.html Miyoshi City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/syuhen-yadani.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.miyoshi.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/35/3515.html Miyoshi City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/syuhen-yadani.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{nihongo|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}''|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.miyoshi.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/35/3515.html Miyoshi City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/syuhen-yadani.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education]{{in lang|ja}}

The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.miyoshi.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/35/3515.html Miyoshi City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/syuhen-yadani.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[:en:natron|natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E|region:JP-34|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-shaped stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[:en:natron|natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[:en:Roman Empire|Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.miyoshi.hiroshima.jp/soshiki/35/3515.html Miyoshi City home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.pref.hiroshima.lg.jp/site/rekimin/syuhen-yadani.html Hiroshima Prefecture Board of Education]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 5]]
}}

The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-type stone coffins, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[:en:natron|natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[:en:Roman Empire|Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yadani Burial Mound]][[en:Yadani Kofun]][[ja:矢谷墳丘墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28688908]] Wikidata name: Yadani Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.78628888888889|N|132.88073055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Miyoshi|qid=Q840591}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-type Sarcophaguss, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[:en:natron|natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[:en:Roman Empire|Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yadani Burial Mound]][[en:Yadani Kofun]][[ja:矢谷墳丘墓]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28688908]] Wikidata name: Yadani Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Yadani Kofun | native_name =矢谷古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Yadani Funkyu-bo, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yadani Kofun | map_type = Japan Hiroshima Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yadani Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.78628888888889|N|132.88073055555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Miyoshi|WD=Q840591}} | region = [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 3rd century AD | abandoned = | epochs =late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|47|10.64|N|132|52|50.63|E}}}}}} [[File:矢谷墳丘墓出土 特殊壺・特殊器台形土器.JPG|right|thumb|red-lacquered vessel stand from the Yadani Kofun]] {{nihongo|'''Yadani Kofun'''|矢谷古墳 |}} is a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] [[Kofun]], located in the Higashisakaya neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Miyoshi, Hiroshima|Miyoshi, Hiroshima]] in the [[:en:San'yō region|San'yō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1979.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162168|title=矢谷古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> Although it is referred to popularly as a ''[[kofun]]'', the Yandani Kofun predates the [[Kofun period]] and its structure has significant differences from Kofuns of the Kofun period.
==Overview== Yatani Kofun is built on a low hill on the south side of the Basen River, which flows into the Miyoshi Basin from the east. It has a total length of 18.5 meters and height of 1.2 meters. The tumulus is located in the midst of the Matsugasakoyaya ruins, a settlement site which was occupied from the [[Jōmon period]] through the [[Kofun period]]. It was discovered in 1977-1979 during the construction of the Miyoshi Industrial Park.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The style of construction is known as {{nihongo|''yosumi-tosshutsugata''|四隅突出形|}}, which is square or rectangular, with protrusions on each of its four corners; however, it is unique in that it is not a true rectangle, but has a narrow waist, similar to a "two conjoined rectangles" type ({{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}). The slope of the mound is covered with ''[[fukiishi]]'' -like stones, with a row of larger stones marking its edge, and is surrounded by a two-meter wide moat. The ''yosumi-tosshutsugata'' style is unique to the late middle Yayoi period and is most prevalent in western Japan in areas influenced by the [[:en:Izumo Province|Izumo culture]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
The mound was found to contain a total of 11 burials, including seven with wooden coffins, two with box-type Sarcophaguss, and an earthen pit burial. The central burial is believed to be one of the wooden coffin burials near the north side of the mound, and jasper tubular beads and small glass beads have been found.<ref name="Shiseki"/> A large number of red-lacquered vessel stands and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]] have been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] from the eastern part of the mound and the surrounding moat. These artifacts have been collectively designated a National [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] and are stored in the Hiroshima Prefectural Museum of History and Folklore.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/198576|title=広島県矢谷古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> From these artifacts, Yadani Kofun is believed to have been constructed from the end of the Yayoi period to the beginning of the Kofun period.
In addition, on the west side of the mound, two square tombs and a [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] kiln site from the Kofun period are included within the National Historic Site boundaries. These smaller Kofuns contained seven wooden coffins, and a number of glass beads. Some of the glass beads excavated from these tombs contain [[:en:natron|natron]], and are attracted attention as products of the ancient [[:en:Roman Empire|Roman Empire]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The Hiroshima Prefectural Miyoshi Fudoki no Oka Park with the [[Jōrakuji-Nanatsuzuka Kofun Cluster]] is about 3 kilometers away to the south-southeast.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Hiroshima)]]
